# Financial Deep Research: Evercore Inc. (EVR)

Research conducted: 2026-05-25  
Stage 1 baseline provided: No — §9 will note the absence.

---

## 1. Recent Financial Performance & Quarterly Trends

### Most Recent Report
Evercore reported record first-quarter 2026 results on April 29, 2026. On a U.S. GAAP basis, net revenues were $1,391.6 million, more than double the $694.8 million posted in Q1 2025 [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Adjusted net revenues were $1,401.5 million. GAAP operating income was $330.7 million (operating margin 23.8%), while adjusted operating income reached $354.5 million (adjusted operating margin 25.3%) [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: earningsrelease1q26.htm / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. GAAP net income attributable to Evercore Inc. was $301.2 million ($7.20 per diluted share), and adjusted net income attributable was $334.7 million ($7.53 per diluted share) [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: earningsrelease1q26.htm / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

The outsized year-over-year advance was driven almost entirely by advisory fees, which surged 123% to $1,244.7 million, fueled by a higher number of large-transaction closings [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Underwriting fees were essentially flat at $55.1 million, while commissions and related revenue rose 14% to $62.7 million. Investment management remained a small contributor, generating $22.8 million in net revenues.

### Most Recent Full Year Report
For the full year 2025, GAAP net revenues were $3,855.8 million (up 29% from $2,979.6 million in 2024), and adjusted net revenues were $3,884.0 million [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. GAAP operating income was $789.9 million (operating margin 20.5%), and adjusted operating income was $838.6 million (adjusted operating margin 21.6%). GAAP net income attributable to common shareholders was $591.9 million ($14.05 per diluted share), while adjusted net income was $646.3 million ($14.56 per diluted share) [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***.

### Key Business Drivers in Recent Quarters
Advisory fee growth has been the dominant driver. In Q1 2026, the firm completed 313 advisory and underwriting client transactions, up from 238 in the prior-year period, with 148 fees of at least $1 million versus 96 a year earlier [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Management highlighted that boardroom sentiment and CEO confidence remain supportive of large-cap strategic M&A, and financing availability is abundant [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. The Private Capital Advisory (PCA) business also contributed to momentum, recognized by industry awards for secondary-market excellence in 2025 [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Segment Performance
Evercore operates two reportable segments: Investment Banking & Equities (IB&E) and Investment Management. IB&E is the overwhelmingly dominant segment.

- **Investment Banking & Equities:** In FY2025, IB&E net revenues were $3,767.7 million (up 30% from $2,898.5 million in 2024). Advisory fees within the segment rose 34% to $3,267.1 million, underwriting fees increased 14% to $179.6 million, and commissions and related revenue increased 13% to $242.7 million [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. In Q1 2026, IB&E generated $1,368.8 million in net revenues versus $674.5 million in Q1 2025 [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Investment Management:** This segment contributed $87.4 million in FY2025 net revenues (up from $79.6 million in FY2024) and $22.8 million in Q1 2026 [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. It remains a marginal part of the overall enterprise.

### Geographic & FX Exposure
Evercore does not disclose precise geographic revenue splits in its quarterly earnings materials. However, management has stated that North American Advisory, EMEA Advisory, Private Capital Advisory, and the Private Funds Group each achieved record revenue years in 2025 [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. The October 2025 acquisition of UK-based Robey Warshaw expanded the EMEA footprint. The firm also noted new senior managing director hires in Asia, including a head of Asia Private Capital Advisory [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Foreign exchange translation impacts are generally modest relative to total revenue, though the firm holds non-dollar-denominated payables and receivables.

---

## 2. Growth Trajectory

### Trajectory Assessment
Because Evercore’s revenue is tightly correlated with global M&A and restructuring activity, a two-year window alone would mislead. The appropriate look-back is five years to capture a full cycle.

- **2021:** Net revenues of approximately $3.29 billion represented a cyclical peak [Source: Company 2022 Annual Report / Title: 2022 Annual Report (shareholder letter) / Date: 2023-02-24] ***.
- **2022:** Net revenues declined to $2.76 billion (down ~16%) as macro headwinds slowed deal flow [Source: Company 2022 Annual Report / Title: 2022 Annual Report (shareholder letter) / Date: 2023-02-24] ***.
- **2023:** Net revenues fell further to approximately $2.44 billion (down ~12%), marking the trough of the recent cycle [Source: Macrotrends / Title: Evercore Inc Revenue 2012-2026 / Date: 2024-12-31] *.
- **2024:** Net revenues rebounded to $2.98 billion (up ~23%) [Source: Company 2024 10-K / Title: Form 10-K for FY2024 / Date: 2025-02-21] ***.
- **2025:** Net revenues reached a record $3.86 billion (up 29%), surpassing the 2021 peak.

This five-year arc demonstrates deep cyclicality. The 2024–2025 recovery has been sharp, and Q1 2026 continued that momentum with 100% adjusted net revenue growth. However, management cautions that quarterly results are “lumpy” due to the timing of large deal closings, and they explicitly declined to extrapolate Q1’s run-rate [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Peer-Relative Growth Context
Evercore’s 29% full-year 2025 revenue growth and 100% Q1 2026 growth compare favorably against the independent advisory peer set. PJT Partners reported 16% revenue growth for the first nine months of 2025 and a record Q3 [Source: PJT Partners Q3 2025 release / Title: PJT Partners Inc. Reports Record Third Quarter and Nine Months 2025 Results / Date: 2025-11-04] **. Houlihan Lokey’s fiscal year 2025 (ended March 2025) revenue grew 25% to $2.39 billion [Source: Houlihan Lokey FY2025 release / Title: Houlihan Lokey Reports Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results / Date: 2025-05-07] **. Moelis & Company’s nine-month 2025 revenues grew roughly in the mid-teens, with a 9-month adjusted compensation ratio of 68.0% [Source: Moelis & Company Q3 2025 release / Title: Moelis & Company Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results / Date: 2025-10-29] **. Evercore appears to be capturing outsized share in the large-cap M&A rebound.

---

## 3. Margin & Profitability Profile

For an investment banking advisory firm, the most meaningful profitability metrics are the **compensation ratio** (employee compensation and benefits as a percentage of net revenues), **operating margin**, and **pre-tax margin**. These reflect the firm’s ability to manage its largest variable cost—talent—while scaling revenue.

- **Compensation Ratio:** Evercore’s adjusted compensation ratio improved to 64.2% for FY2025 (down from 65.7% in FY2024) and was 64.0% in Q1 2026 (down from 65.7% in Q1 2025) [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***; [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Management attributed the improvement to revenue scale and market-share gains, partially offset by continued investment in talent.
- **Operating Margin:** Adjusted operating margin expanded to 21.6% in FY2025 (from 18.6% in FY2024) and 25.3% in Q1 2026 (from 16.6% in Q1 2025) [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***; [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: earningsrelease1q26.htm / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
- **Tax Rate:** The adjusted effective tax rate was 19.8% in FY2025 versus 21.8% in FY2024, principally reflecting deductions from RSU vesting above original grant prices [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. In Q1 2026, the adjusted tax rate was an anomalously low 3.0% due to the same dynamic; management does not guide to this recurring [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Peer-Relative Profitability Context
Evercore’s adjusted compensation ratio of 64.2% in FY2025 sits below PJT Partners (67.1% for FY2025) and Moelis (68.0% for the first nine months of 2025) [Source: MarketBeat / Title: PJT Partners Q4 2025 Earnings Report / Date: 2026-02-03] *; [Source: Moelis & Company Q3 2025 release / Title: Moelis & Company Reports Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results / Date: 2025-10-29] **. Houlihan Lokey’s FY2025 compensation ratio was approximately 63.8% [Source: Houlihan Lokey FY2025 release / Title: Houlihan Lokey Reports Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results / Date: 2025-05-07] **. Evercore’s operating margin is currently at the high end of the independent advisory peer group, suggesting superior operating leverage or a richer mix of large-cap advisory fees.

---

## 4. Cash Generation & Quality

### Cash Generation Quality Assessment
Evercore’s business model generates strong cash flow, but with pronounced seasonality. For FY2025, net cash provided by operating activities was $1,256.5 million, up from $988.2 million in FY2024 [Source: Company 2025 Annual Report / Title: 2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Capital expenditures are minimal—primarily office build-outs and technology hardware—so free cash flow closely tracks operating cash flow. The firm does not report a formal free cash flow metric, but deducting estimated capex of roughly $30–$50 million (implied by investing activities net of acquisition and securities activity) implies free cash flow conversion well in excess of net income.

In Q1 2026, operating cash flow was negative $225.9 million, a predictable seasonal outflow driven by the payment of prior-year bonus awards and deferred cash compensation [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***. This is structurally normal: the firm traditionally pays employee bonuses in the first quarter.

### Working Capital & Cash Conversion
Working capital dynamics are atypical for an operating company. The largest current asset is cash and investment securities ($2.0 billion combined at March 31, 2026); the largest current liability is accrued compensation and benefits ($653.3 million at March 31, 2026, down from $1,381.3 million at year-end) [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***. Accounts receivable turnover is generally within 90 days, with the exception of certain private capital advisory placement fees collected within 180 days and select private funds capital-raising fees that may extend beyond one year [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. The balance sheet is asset-light; inventory and traditional trade payables are immaterial.

### Earnings Quality
Earnings quality appears high in the sense that full-year operating cash flow consistently exceeds GAAP net income, driven largely by non-cash equity-based compensation add-backs. However, analysts should note that:
1. **Adjusted results** exclude acquisition-related compensation, intangible amortization, and contingent consideration marks related to Robey Warshaw. In Q1 2026, these items totaled approximately $13.8 million on a pre-tax basis [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: earningsrelease1q26.htm / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
2. **Tax-rate volatility** can distort net income quarter-to-quarter. The Q1 2026 GAAP tax provision was only $9.1 million on $331.8 million in pre-tax income, producing an effective rate of ~2.7%. This is non-recurring and tied to share-price appreciation on vested RSUs [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: earningsrelease1q26.htm / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

---

## 5. Balance Sheet & Liquidity

As of March 31, 2026, Evercore’s balance sheet reflected:
- **Cash and Cash Equivalents:** $986.0 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Investment Securities and Certificates of Deposit:** $1,035.0 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Total Current Assets:** $2,810.6 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Total Assets:** $4,314.3 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Total Liabilities:** $2,225.7 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Total Evercore Inc. Stockholders’ Equity:** $1,782.1 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.
- **Total Equity (including noncontrolling interests):** $2,088.6 million [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***.

The decline in total assets and liabilities from year-end ($5.36 billion and $3.04 billion, respectively) is seasonal, driven by the Q1 drawdown of accrued compensation [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***.

### Debt Maturity Profile & Refinancing Risk
Total notes payable were $539.7 million at March 31, 2026, down from $588.2 million at December 31, 2025, reflecting the $48.0 million repayment of Series C notes at maturity [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***; [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. The remaining scheduled maturities are:

- **Series D:** ~$16.9 million, due March 2028
- **Series J:** ~$66.1 million, due November 2028
- **Series E:** ~$74.6 million, due August 2029
- **Series K:** $125.0 million, due July 2030
- **Series F:** ~$59.6 million, due August 2031
- **Series L:** $125.0 million, due July 2032
- **Series G:** ~$39.7 million, due August 2033
- **Series H:** ~$32.1 million, due August 2033

[Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20250331 (historical note schedule) / Date: 2025-05-06] ***; [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***.

In addition, the firm maintains an undrawn $225.0 million revolving credit facility maturing July 2028 and an undrawn $75.0 million subordinated revolving facility maturing October 2029 [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. There are no near-term maturities of size, and the nearest material maturity is not until 2028. Interest rates on the notes are fixed, ranging from ~3.33% to ~5.58% (with the 2025 issuances at 5.17% and 5.47%).

### Assessment
Balance-sheet risk is low. The firm holds roughly $2.0 billion in liquid assets (cash plus short-term investments) against $539.7 million in notes payable, implying a net cash position [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: evr-20260331 / Date: 2026-05-06] ***; [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Leverage is minimal for a financial services firm, and refinancing risk is negligible over the next two years.

---

## 6. Capital Deployment & Returns on Capital

### Share Repurchases
Evercore has prioritized repurchases as the primary offset to RSU dilution. In FY2025, the firm returned $812.4 million to shareholders through dividends and repurchases of 2.4 million shares at an average price of $275.42 [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Form 8-K / Date: 2026-02-04] ***. In Q1 2026, the firm returned a record $673.3 million, including repurchases of 1.9 million shares at an average price of $322.00 (0.9 million shares for net RSU settlement at $344.71 and 1.0 million via open-market repurchases at $302.01) [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Management highlighted that the firm has repurchased shares in excess of RSU issuance for six consecutive years [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Dividends
The quarterly dividend was increased 6% to $0.89 per share in Q1 2026, up from $0.84 [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. At the current share count, the annualized dividend commitment is roughly $138 million. The payout ratio relative to GAAP earnings is approximately 20–25%, leaving ample room for buybacks.

### M&A / Capex
The only material recent acquisition is Robey Warshaw, which closed on October 1, 2025. Total consideration includes upfront cash and stock of roughly $101 million, a first-anniversary payment of ~£74.8 million ($100.8 million), and contingent consideration with a fair value of ~$25.3 million [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. The transaction added $102.2 million of goodwill and $33.7 million of intangible assets. Management has stated that strategic acquisitions are a “very high bar” and not a capital allocation priority relative to talent recruitment [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. Capital expenditures are minimal—estimated at roughly $30–$50 million annually.

### Returns on Capital
Evercore does not report return on tangible equity (ROTE) or return on invested capital (ROIC) in its earnings materials. Using GAAP net income attributable to Evercore Inc. of $591.9 million and average stockholders’ equity of approximately $1,870 million (averaging $1,707.6 million at Dec. 31, 2024 and $2,031.6 million at Dec. 31, 2025), implied ROE for FY2025 is roughly **32%** [Source: Company FY2025 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Revenues / Date: 2026-02-04] ***; [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. On an adjusted basis, ROE approaches **35%**. These are elevated levels, supported by the cyclical revenue upswing and modest equity base. Whether these returns on incremental capital are sustainable depends on maintaining the current revenue run-rate without a proportional increase in headcount or compensation guarantees.

---

## 7. Management Guidance & Forward Outlook

### Most Recent Full Year Report Guidance
Evercore does not issue formal annual revenue or earnings guidance. Instead, management provides qualitative commentary on the business environment and expense expectations.

### Most Recent Report Guidance
On the Q1 2026 call, CEO John Weinberg and CFO Timothy LaLonde offered the following directional outlook:
- **Q2 2026 Revenue:** Management expects Q2 2026 to be “closer to what we experienced in last year's second quarter, which was a record” [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. This suggests revenue likely in the vicinity of Q2 2025 levels (which were not separately disclosed, but full-year 2025 quarterly trends imply a strong comparable).
- **Compensation Ratio:** Further improvement in 2026 will likely be “meaningfully more modest” than in 2024 and 2025 [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
- **Non-Compensation Expenses:** Management expects a similar growth rate in non-comps in 2026, in line with the last couple of years (up ~17% in FY2025), driven by technology, professional fees, and travel [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
- **Capital Return:** Dividend growth and sustained share buybacks are described as ongoing commitments [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Management Commentary
Management emphasized that CEO confidence is “very sound and quite high,” boardrooms are active, and financing markets are receptive for large-cap strategic M&A [Source: Company Q1 earnings call / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. They also cautioned that large-cap activity increases quarterly revenue variability. Technology and AI investments are viewed as internally focused (productivity) and externally oriented (client advisory).

### Forward Claim Confidence Weighting
| Forward Claim | Confidence | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Q2 2026 revenue near prior-year record | **Medium** | Management has visibility into near-term closings, but advisory revenue is inherently lumpy and deal slippage is common. |
| Compensation ratio improvement modest in 2026 | **High** | Explicit management guidance; 64% is already near the low end of peer range, leaving limited runway. |
| Non-compensation expense growth in line with recent years | **Medium** | Management has controlled this line well, but inflation in technology licensing and travel could surprise. |
| Capital returns (dividends + buybacks) remain a priority | **High** | Six-year track record of buybacks exceeding RSU issuance; dividend was just raised. |
| Large-cap M&A environment remains supportive | **Medium** | Macro and financing conditions appear supportive, but subject to rapid shifts in rates, regulation, or geopolitics. |

## 8. Material Financial Risks & One-Off Items

- **Cyclical Revenue Concentration:** Approximately 85% of net revenues are derived from advisory fees. A downturn in M&A or restructuring activity would disproportionately impact results. This is the single largest financial risk.
- **Talent Retention and Compensation Inflation:** The investment banking labor market is competitive. Evercore must continually grant RSUs and deferred cash to retain senior managing directors. As of March 31, 2026, there were 4.5 million unvested RSUs with an aggregate grant date fair value of $1.1 billion, plus $295.1 million in deferred cash compensation obligations through 2030 [Source: Company Q1 earnings release / Title: Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
- **Robey Warshaw Integration and Earn-Outs:** The acquisition adds contingent consideration and performance-based compensation. While the financial impact is modest relative to firm-wide revenue, earn-out accounting creates potential volatility in reported expenses.
- **Tax Rate Volatility:** The effective tax rate is sensitive to Evercore’s stock price at RSU vesting dates. A decline in the share price could reduce the tax deduction and raise the effective rate materially.
- **Receivables and Credit Risk:** While bad debt expense is typically small ($5.6 million in FY2025), a portion of receivables relates to private funds capital raising and may extend beyond one year [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***. A slowdown in private markets could delay collections.
- **Interest Rate Sensitivity:** Higher rates increase interest income on cash balances but also raise interest expense on fixed-rate debt. Net interest expense was $24.3 million in FY2025, up from $16.8 million in FY2024, reflecting the July 2025 senior note issuance [Source: Company 10-K / Title: FY2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-02-12] ***.

## 9. Discrepancies vs. Structured Data Baseline

### Research Data vs. Stage 1 Baseline
**Stage 1 baseline not provided at runtime — section deferred.** All data points in this report were sourced independently via SEC filings, company press releases, earnings call transcripts, and financial data providers. If a Stage 1 baseline is furnished downstream, the following items should be reconciled: FY2025 revenue, operating income, compensation ratio, shares outstanding, cash and debt balances, and Q1 2026 EPS. Any material divergence from independently sourced figures should be investigated for differences in GAAP vs. adjusted presentation, fiscal period definitions, or share-count methodology (basic vs. diluted).

## 10. Key Financial Assumptions Embedded in Current Valuation

As of May 22, 2026, Evercore Class A shares traded at approximately $346.12, with a market capitalization of roughly $13.4 billion (38.68 million shares outstanding) and a trailing P/E ratio near 19.5× [Source: Google Finance / Title: EVR Stock Price & News / Date: 2026-05-23] *; [Source: Google Finance / Title: EVR Stock Price & News / Date: 2026-05-23] *. The following financial assumptions appear embedded in this valuation:

1. **Sustained Earnings Power:** A TTM P/E of ~19.5× implies the market believes the Q1 2026 earnings spike is not purely cyclical but reflects a higher run-rate of profitability. This requires large-cap advisory activity to remain elevated through 2026 and beyond.
2. **Compensation Ratio Stability:** The market appears to assume Evercore can hold the compensation ratio near 64% (or better) without losing key revenue producers to competitors.
3. **Operating Margin in the Low-to-Mid 20s:** The current multiple prices in continued operating leverage, with adjusted margins comfortably above 20%.
4. **Capital Return Velocity:** The valuation likely assumes the firm will continue returning the majority of free cash flow to shareholders via buybacks and dividends, with repurchases consistently exceeding RSU dilution.
5. **Normalized Tax Rate:** The market must be looking through the Q1 2026 tax anomaly (3% rate) and assuming a normalized effective rate in the high teens to low 20s.
6. **Successful Robey Warshaw Integration:** The earn-out and retention awards must not erode the deal’s economic value, and the UK franchise must contribute to EMEA growth.

---

## 11. Bottom-Line Financial Assessment

### Business Model Characterization
Evercore is a **human-capital-intensive, asset-light, cyclical advisory franchise** with a concentrated revenue stream in large-cap M&A and strategic advisory. It operates with minimal fixed asset requirements, negligible inventory, and a compensation-heavy cost structure that functions as a variable cost over the medium term. Financial analysis therefore centers on **revenue per senior managing director, compensation ratio, operating margin, and return on equity** rather than traditional manufacturing or lending metrics. Cash flow is robust but seasonally distorted by Q1 bonus payments.

### Strengths (confidence weighted)
- **Revenue momentum and market-share gains in large-cap advisory** — *High confidence.* FY2025 and Q1 2026 results show outsized growth versus peers, with record fees from transactions over $1 million.
- **Operating leverage and margin expansion** — *High confidence.* The adjusted compensation ratio has declined for two consecutive years, and the operating margin expanded 300 basis points from FY2024 to FY2025.
- **Fortress balance sheet with net cash** — *High confidence.* Liquid assets exceed total debt by a wide margin, and near-term refinancing risk is negligible.
- **Disciplined, anti-dilutive capital return program** — *High confidence.* Six consecutive years of repurchases exceeding RSU issuance, coupled with consistent dividend growth.
- **Diversification within advisory** — *Medium confidence.* Private Capital Advisory, restructuring, and equity underwriting provide partial offsets if pure M&A slows, though advisory still dominates.

### Weaknesses (confidence weighted)
- **Extreme revenue concentration in advisory fees** — *High confidence.* Roughly 85% of net revenues are advisory-driven, leaving the firm exposed to M&A cycle turns.
- **Compensation inflation risk and talent wars** — *High confidence.* RSU overhang and deferred cash obligations are large ($1.1 billion unvested RSUs; $295 million deferred cash), and peer firms are aggressively hiring.
- **Cyclicality and quarterly lumpiness** — *High confidence.* Management explicitly warns against extrapolating any single quarter; Q1 2026 benefitted from a surge in large deal closings that may not repeat.
- **Non-recurring tax benefits flattering near-term earnings** — *High confidence.* The Q1 2026 effective tax rate of ~3% is unsustainable and added roughly $90+ million in after-tax income versus a normalized rate.

### Watch Items
- **Q2 2026 revenue comparables:** Management’s comment that Q2 will be “closer to last year's record” is a key near-term benchmark. A significant miss would challenge the “sustained run-rate” narrative embedded in the P/E multiple.
- **Senior managing director headcount productivity:** SMD count grew from 157 to 182 year-over-year in investment banking. Revenue per SMD must remain high to justify the fixed cost of talent.
- **Accrued compensation and cash flow seasonality:** The Q1 drawdown of accrued comp reduced current assets and liabilities sharply. Monitor whether FY2026 year-end accruals rebuild in line with revenue.
- **Robey Warshaw earn-out performance:** Contingent consideration of ~$25 million is tied to multi-year performance thresholds. Underperformance could trigger write-backs or, conversely, outperformance could require higher payouts.
- **Non-compensation expense growth:** Management has guided to continued growth in technology and travel costs. If this outpaces revenue, margin expansion stalls.

---

## 12. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Geographic revenue breakdown:** No data found for precise North America / EMEA / Asia revenue splits; search scope: company 10-K, earnings releases, and earnings supplements. Analytical impact: limits ability to assess regional concentration risks.
- **Exact FY2025 capital expenditures:** No explicit capex line item was located in the FY2025 10-K summary; search scope: 10-K cash flow statement, earnings release, supplemental materials. Analytical impact: free cash flow estimates rely on inferred capex from investing activity descriptions.
- **Q2 2025 historical EPS/Revenue:** Exact quarterly figures for Q2 2025 were not independently sourced; search scope: SEC filings, earnings release archives. Analytical impact: weakens precision when benchmarking management’s Q2 2026 guidance.

# News & Narrative Deep Research: Evercore Inc. (EVR)

Research conducted: 2026-05-25

## 1. Most Significant News in the Window

### Q1 2026 Record Earnings Met with Investor Skepticism
The dominant market-moving event in the window was Evercore’s first-quarter 2026 earnings release on April 29, 2026. The firm reported record adjusted net revenues of approximately $1.4 billion, up 100% year-over-year and 8% sequentially, driven by a surge in large-cap M&A closings. Adjusted earnings per share were $7.53, beating consensus estimates by roughly 45–47% depending on the analyst benchmark. Adjusted advisory fees hit a record $1.2 billion, rising 123% year-over-year. [Source: Company press release / Title: "Evercore Reports Record First Quarter 2026 Revenues; Increases Quarterly Dividend to $0.89 Per Share" / Date: 2026-04-29 ***] Despite the blowout results, the stock declined approximately 2.1% intraday to around $340.51, signaling that the market treated the quarter as a potential cyclical peak rather than a new run-rate. [Source: AlphaStreet / Title: "Evercore Q1 2026 EPS Tops Expectations by 46.8%, Revenue Up 100%" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

### Capital Return Hits Quarterly Record
Concurrent with earnings, Evercore announced a record $673 million in quarterly capital returns, comprising 1.9 million shares repurchased and dividend payments. The board raised the quarterly dividend by 6% to $0.89 per share. The blended average repurchase price was $322, with roughly 900,000 shares acquired via net settlement of vesting RSUs at $345 and approximately 1 million shares bought in the open market at $302. Adjusted diluted shares outstanding fell by over 500,000 sequentially to 44.4 million. [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

### League Table Surge Reinforces Structural Share Gain
In early April, Q1 2026 global M&A league table data showed Evercore surging to #6 by deal value (Dealogic data, $108.7 billion across 54 deals) from #12 in full-year 2025, implying an average deal size exceeding $2 billion. A separate league table compilation placed Evercore at #5 with $137.6 billion in volume, up 56% year-over-year. While the exact rank differs by data provider, the directional story is consistent: Evercore is capturing disproportionate share of large-cap strategic advisory. [Source: LinkedIn / Title: "Evercore Surges to #6 in Q1 2026 M&A League Tables" / Date: 2026-04-02] [Source: ionanalytics / Title: "Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan lead 1Q26 M&A rankings amid relentless hunt for scale" / Date: 2026-04-01 **] *Note: These two sources differ on exact rank and volume due to differing methodologies and date cutoffs; both confirm a top-6 placement and significant year-over-year increase.*

### Post-Earnings Senior Hiring Flurry
In May 2026, Evercore announced a trio of senior managing director hires: Clay McCoy joined the Private Capital Advisory group on May 19; Chris Connelly joined the Industrials Investment Banking group on May 18; and Mark Hibbert joined Evercore Equities as a senior sales trader on May 12. The firm also flagged its inaugural TMT Conference scheduled for June 2–3, 2026, and its sixth annual Consumer & Retail Conference for June 9–11, 2026. [Source: Company press release / Title: "Clay McCoy Joins Evercore as Senior Managing Director in Private Capital Advisory" / Date: 2026-05-19 ***] [Source: Company press release / Title: "Chris Connelly Joins Evercore as Senior Managing Director in the Industrials Investment Banking Group" / Date: 2026-05-18 ***] [Source: Company press release / Title: "Mark Hibbert Joins Evercore Equities as Senior Managing Director" / Date: 2026-05-12 ***]

## 2. Earnings Call Deep Dive

### Headline Takeaways
The April 29, 2026 call underscored record performance across nearly every segment: North American Advisory, EMEA Advisory, Private Capital Advisory (PCA), Private Funds Group (PFG), Equities, and Wealth Management all posted record first-quarter revenues. Notable advisory roles included Warner Bros. Discovery’s $110 billion sale, Devon Energy’s $58 billion merger, and Jetro Restaurant Depot’s $29 billion sale to Sysco. Management emphasized that the quarter featured the highest number of large-transaction closings in firm history, driven by both delayed and accelerated deal completions. [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

### Management Tone & Conviction
Chairman and CEO John Weinberg struck a tone of quiet confidence laced with deliberate caution. On the macro environment, he stated: *"We are seeing continued CEO and boardroom confidence, particularly around large-cap transactions, and financing markets are open. However, conditions have become more mixed in recent months."* [Source: AOL / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] He later added: *"While ongoing geopolitical and macroeconomic uncertainty could extend transaction time lines if it persists throughout the year, we believe the underlying conditions for a strong M&A environment remain, albeit with some bumps along the way."* [Source: AOL / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

Crucially, Weinberg explicitly warned against extrapolation: *"As we have noted in the past, investors should not place too much emphasis on any one quarter. This holds true in very strong quarters as well as challenging ones. And we would encourage you not to extrapolate these results."* [Source: AOL / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] This phrase appears to have been interpreted by the market as a signal that Q1’s exceptional close rate was unlikely to repeat sequentially.

CFO Tim LaLonde highlighted capital return discipline and expense leverage. The adjusted compensation ratio improved to 64.0%, down from 65.7% in Q1 2025, while the non-compensation ratio compressed to 10.7% from 17.7%. LaLonde also noted that the effective tax rate was unusually low in Q1 (3.0% adjusted) due to tax benefits from RSU vesting above grant prices, and he cautioned that the remaining quarters would normalize toward historical rates. [Source: MarketBeat / Title: "Evercore Q1 2026 Earnings Report" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

### Q&A Pushback & Analyst Skepticism
Analysts pressed management on five distinct areas of concern, as summarized in post-call research notes and transcript excerpts:

- **Software sector stress (KBW):** Alex Bond asked how stress and lower public-market valuations in software were affecting M&A and restructuring activity. Weinberg acknowledged a slowdown but pushed back on the idea of a freeze: *"There's definitely a slowdown, but it's not a standstill."* He noted that some situations had slowed substantially while others presented consolidation opportunities. [Source: AOL / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

- **European regulatory headwinds (Morgan Stanley):** Ryan Kenny inquired about Evercore’s European growth amid potential EU merger-rule changes. Weinberg responded that EMEA delivered a record first quarter and that the firm was seeing increased boardroom engagement despite regulatory overhang. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: "5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Evercore's Q1 Earnings Call" / Date: 2026-05-06 **]

- **Middle-market sponsor sluggishness (Seaport Global):** James Mitchell questioned the slow rebound in sponsor-led middle-market activity. Weinberg noted that middle-market deals had slowed but said Evercore’s pitch and win rates were up, reflecting an expanded sponsor-focused offering. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: "5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Evercore's Q1 Earnings Call" / Date: 2026-05-06 **]

- **Compensation ratio sustainability (BMO Capital Markets):** Brennan Hawken asked about compensation trends and talent competition. According to the call summary, LaLonde indicated that compensation ratio improvements may be more modest going forward while reaffirming investment in high-value talent. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: "5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Evercore's Q1 Earnings Call" / Date: 2026-05-06 **]

- **Large-cap M&A durability (Goldman Sachs):** James Yaro sought clarity on whether large-cap activity was sustainable given regulatory risk. Weinberg said large deals remained welcome in the current environment but cautioned that regulatory dynamics could shift after the U.S. midterms. [Source: The Globe and Mail / Title: "5 Must-Read Analyst Questions From Evercore's Q1 Earnings Call" / Date: 2026-05-06 **]

### Forward Commentary & Guidance
Management did not provide explicit quantitative quarterly guidance but framed the first half outlook in qualitative terms. LaLonde stated: *"In aggregate, we believe our first half will reflect continued strong performance, and we remain enthusiastic about the outlook for our business."* [Source: MarketBeat / Title: "Evercore Q1 2026 Earnings Report" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] A Seeking Alpha headline summarizing the call noted that Evercore signaled Q2 would be *"closer to last year's record"* following the Q1 surge and deal-timing shifts. [Source: Seeking Alpha / Title: "Evercore signals second quarter to be closer to last year's record following $1.4B Q1 and deal timing shifts" / Date: 2026-04-29 *]

Weinberg also emphasized backlog strength: *"Our backlog remains strong and is replenishing at a healthy rate."* [Source: AOL / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] Despite this, the confluence of "don't extrapolate" language, a normalized tax-rate warning, and "mixed" macro commentary appeared to offset the record results in investors' minds.

## 3. Analyst Coverage & Rating Changes

### Consensus Picture
As of late May 2026, sell-side coverage remains constructive but divided between Buy and Hold recommendations. Data compiled on May 22, 2026, show a consensus price target of approximately $347.25 based on eight analysts, with a range spanning from roughly $330 (UBS) to $420 (Keefe, Bruyette & Woods). [Source: Public.com / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Stock Forecast: Analyst Ratings, Predictions & Price Target 2026" / Date: 2026-05-22] A separate Finviz snapshot dated May 8, 2026, lists a target price of $380.11 with a recommendation score of 1.83 (closer to Buy than Hold). [Source: Finviz / Title: "EVR - Evercore Inc. Stock Quote" / Date: 2026-05-08 *] *Note: The discrepancy likely reflects different sample sizes and timing; the Public.com consensus is the most recent dated snapshot available.*

Management emphasized that the board remains committed to offsetting bonus-related RSU dilution. [Source: The Motley Fool / Title: "Evercore (EVR) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] The market narrative around this capital return appears bifurcated: some investors read it as a confidence signal, while others view the elevated buyback activity as a substitute for reinvestment in a maturing cycle.

### Institutional Sentiment

Notable moves included exits by Bain Capital Public Equity Management (complete removal of 434,209 shares) and Capital International Investors (72% reduction), while FMR LLC added 405,800 shares (+47.7%), Millennium Management added 291,575 shares (+119.0%), and Holocene Advisors initiated a significant position (+229,674 shares). ### Retail & Social Sentiment
Retail and social media sentiment does not appear to be a material driver of Evercore’s stock narrative in this window. The ticker lacks the elevated discussion volume on Reddit, Twitter, or StockTwits that would indicate a retail-driven narrative. This is assessed as lower-signal noise relative to institutional positioning.

### Short Interest
Short interest has risen modestly but remains low. As of the most recent reporting period, short interest stood at approximately 1.06 million shares, up from roughly 903,000 previously, representing about 2.8–2.95% of the float. [Source: Finviz / Title: "EVR - Evercore Inc. Stock Quote" / Date: 2026-05-08 *] [Source: Benzinga / Title: "Evercore Stock Short Interest Report" / Date: Most recent reporting period prior to May 2026 *] The trend suggests a slight increase in bearish positioning post-earnings, but the absolute level remains insufficient to drive price action independently.

### Valuation Narrative
The market is actively debating whether Evercore deserves a premium multiple or is approaching a cyclical peak. Following the Q1 beat, the stock trades at roughly 17–19x trailing earnings and approximately 15x forward earnings. One camp argues the boutique model, league-table ascent, and large-cap advisory mix justify a premium; the other camp worries that advisory revenue is inherently lumpy, Q1 benefited from pulled-forward closings, and the compensation ratio cannot improve indefinitely. [Source: AlphaStreet / Title: "Evercore Q1 2026 EPS Tops Expectations by 46.8%, Revenue Up 100%" / Date: 2026-04-29 *] [Source: Finviz / Title: "EVR - Evercore Inc. Stock Quote" / Date: 2026-05-08 *]

## 7. Short-Seller Research & Activist Investor Involvement

No short-seller reports or activist investor activity were identified in the research window.

## 8. Narrative Shifts

A discernible narrative shift occurred during and immediately after the April 29, 2026, earnings release.

- **Prior narrative:** Evercore was a leveraged recovery play on the rebound in M&A activity, with investors willing to bid the stock higher on expectation that advisory volumes would normalize toward 2021–2022 levels.
- **Current narrative:** Evercore is now increasingly framed as a "cyclical peak" story, where the underlying business is executing exceptionally well but the stock may have discounted a sustained high level of deal flow that management itself warns against extrapolating.
- **Trigger:** The specific trigger was the combination of a record quarterly beat with a down stock reaction, amplified by Weinberg’s direct quote—*"we would encourage you not to extrapolate these results"*—and LaLonde’s tax-rate normalization warning. The market’s reaction function shifted from rewarding beats to questioning sustainability.

## 9. Narrative-vs-Reality Disconnects

A material disconnect appears to exist between the structural share gains Evercore is demonstrating and the market’s cyclical-peak pricing of the stock.

- **Observable reality:** Evercore surged to a top-6 global M&A advisory ranking in Q1 2026, is capturing lead roles on megadeals (average deal size >$2 billion), continues to hire senior talent, returned record capital, and maintains a strong replenishing backlog. These are characteristics of a firm gaining durable share, not merely riding a wave.
- **Dominant market pricing:** The stock sold off on record earnings and trades at a mid-teens forward multiple, behavior more consistent with a business at cyclical highs. If the large-cap advisory share gains prove sticky, the market may be underpricing the durability of earnings power.

## 10. Bottom-Line Narrative Assessment

### Narrative Characterization
Evercore is currently viewed as a best-in-class independent advisory franchise executing at the top of its cycle. The market acknowledges its structural share gains and capital return discipline but is reluctant to award a higher multiple due to fears that Q1 2026 represented a cyclical high-water mark for M&A closings. The prevailing story is one of exceptional execution trapped inside a skeptical macro narrative.

### Key Narrative Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **Large-cap M&A cycle positioning** — High confidence. The Q1 league-table surge and backlog commentary from management support this as the primary driver of near-term revenue and sentiment.
2. **Structural boutique share gain** — High confidence. Evercore’s ascent in global rankings and average deal size demonstrate a move upmarket that is widely cited by both management and sell-side analysts.
3. **Capital return and compensation discipline** — Medium-High confidence. The record $673 million quarterly return and 64% comp ratio are viewed positively, though analysts question how much further the ratio can fall.
4. **Talent retention and senior banker ramp** — Medium confidence. The 182 SMD count and 45+ ramping bankers are bullish data points, but compensation inflation and boutique competition remain latent risks.
5. **Software/tech advisory slowdown** — Medium confidence. Acknowledged by Weinberg as a real but contained headwind; not yet large enough to derail the overall narrative.

### Narrative Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **Deal flow moderation in H2 2026** — High confidence. This is the single greatest fragility in the bull case. If large-cap closings dry up or financing markets close, the stock is vulnerable to rapid multiple compression.
2. **Regulatory interference on large transactions** — Medium confidence. Analysts (Goldman Sachs) and management have both flagged that U.S. midterm dynamics and EU merger-rule reviews could alter the landscape.
3. **Compensation ratio reversion** — Medium confidence. The Q1 tax benefit is non-recurring, and LaLonde signaled that comp-ratio improvements may moderate as talent competition persists.
4. **Geopolitical escalation** — Medium confidence. Weinberg explicitly noted that geopolitical uncertainty could extend transaction timelines.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **Q2 2026 Earnings Release:** Expected late July 2026; will test whether advisory revenues can remain near "last year's record" Q2 level as management suggested.
- **Evercore Global TMT Conference:** June 2–3, 2026 in San Francisco. Management commentary on tech/software deal pipelines could move sentiment.
- **Evercore Consumer & Retail Conference:** June 9–11, 2026 in New York.
- **Ex-Dividend Date:** May 29, 2026 for the $0.89 quarterly dividend. [Source: Finviz / Title: "EVR - Evercore Inc. Stock Quote" / Date: 2026-05-08 *]

## 11. Data Gaps & Limitations

- **Q1 2026 13F institutional holdings data:** Not fully available as of the research date; the most recent granular 13F data covers Q4 2025, creating a roughly 90-day lag in assessing post-earnings institutional positioning. Search scope: Quiver Quantitative, Fintel, SEC EDGAR 13F filings.

Search scope: Seeking Alpha, Bloomberg, broker research aggregators.

# Competitive & Macro Deep Research: Evercore Inc. (EVR)

Research conducted: 2026-05-25

## 1. Competitive Position & Market Share

### Peer Set & Justification
The relevant peer set for Evercore’s competitive position is **elite independent (boutique) investment banks**: Lazard, Inc. (LAZ), Moelis & Company (MC), Houlihan Lokey, Inc. (HLI), Centerview Partners, PJT Partners, and Rothschild & Co. These firms share Evercore’s conflict-free, capital-light model—no commercial banking, large balance-sheet lending, or proprietary trading—and compete directly for board-level advisory mandates, mega-cap M&A, and financial sponsor engagements. Bulge-bracket institutions (Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley) are not direct business-model peers, but they remain the primary competitive frame because they dominate global deal volume and often compete for the same lead advisory roles.

### Market Share & Position
Evercore characterizes itself as the **leading independent investment banking firm globally based on the dollar volume of announced worldwide M&A transactions over the preceding five years** [Source: Company 10-K / Title: 2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-30] ***. In Q1 2026, this positioning translated into an exceptional quarter: the firm reported advisory fees of **$1.245 billion** (up 123% year-over-year) and total net revenues of **$1.392 billion** (up 100% year-over-year) [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

By announced deal value, Evercore surged to **#6 globally** in Q1 2026 with **$108.7 billion** across 54 deals, jumping from #12 in full-year 2025 [Source: LinkedIn / Title: "Evercore Surges to #6 in Q1 2026 M&A League Tables" referencing Dealogic / Date: 2026-04-02] *. In the Americas, Evercore ranked #7 with an **8.5% share** ($47.1 billion, 35 deals) [Source: Wall Street Oasis / Title: "2026 Q1 Americas M&A League Table Standings" / Date: 2026-03-19] *. These league table figures are single-quarter snapshots and are inherently volatile; the five-year volume claim in the 10-K is a more durable indicator of structural position. Evercore’s average deal size in Q1 2026 exceeded $2 billion, suggesting the firm is winning the largest, most complex mandates where advisory fees are concentrated.

### Relative Positioning
Evercore is currently outperforming its independent peers by a wide margin. In Q1 2026, Lazard’s Financial Advisory segment generated **$356 million** in adjusted net revenue, down 4% year-over-year [Source: Lazard Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-05-01] ***. Moelis & Company reported total Q1 2026 revenue of **$319.8 million**, up only 4% year-over-year [Source: TradingView / Title: Moelis & Co 1Q 2026 10-Q Summary / Date: 2026-04-30] **. Houlihan Lokey—stronger in mid-market corporate finance and restructuring—generated **$398.5 million** in Corporate Finance revenue for its fiscal quarter ended June 30, 2025, up 21% year-over-year, but remains roughly one-third the scale of Evercore’s advisory business on an annualized basis [Source: Houlihan Lokey Investor Relations / Title: Q1 Fiscal 2026 Financial Results / Date: 2025-07-29] ***.

Relative to bulge brackets, Evercore cannot match the breadth of JPMorgan or Goldman Sachs, which topped Q1 2026 global league tables with $265.7 billion and $424.8 billion in announced deal value, respectively [Source: LinkedIn / Title: "Evercore Surges to #6 in Q1 2026 M&A League Tables" referencing Dealogic / Date: 2026-04-02] *. However, Evercore’s value proposition is not breadth but **purity of advice**: the absence of lending and trading conflicts allows it to win contested board mandates, special committee roles, and complex cross-border situations where objectivity is paramount.

## 2. Competitive Moat

### Moat Sources
Evercore’s moat rests on four specific, interlocking sources:

1. **Independence and Conflict-Free Positioning**. Evercore does not engage in commercial banking, significant proprietary trading, or balance-sheet lending. This eliminates the cross-selling conflicts that plague universal banks and is the foundational pitch to corporate boards and special committees [Source: Company 10-K / Title: 2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-30] ***.
2. **Reputation and Track Record in Mega-Cap M&A**. Landmark transactions—such as advising on Warner Bros. Discovery’s $110 billion sale and Devon Energy’s $58 billion merger in Q1 2026—reinforce a brand associated with the highest-stakes, most complex deals [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.
3. **Human Capital and Client Relationships**. The firm employed **182 Investment Banking Senior Managing Directors** as of early 2026, up from 171 at the start of the year [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-04-29] ***. In advisory banking, client stickiness often resides with the individual banker, and Evercore’s ability to attract and retain senior rainmakers is central to its franchise.
4. **Scale Economies Within the Boutique Segment**. Evercore’s global platform—spanning the Americas, EMEA (expanded via the Robey Warshaw acquisition to nine countries and over 400 professionals in the region), and Asia-Pacific—provides sector coverage and geographic reach that smaller boutiques cannot match [Source: Financial Times / Title: "Evercore buys London’s elite M&A boutique Robey Warshaw for $196mn" / Date: 2025-07-30] **.

### Moat Durability Assessment
- **Independence**: **Strengthening, durable**. Regulatory and shareholder scrutiny of bulge-bracket conflicts has not abated. As long as universal banks maintain integrated models, the independent advisory segment will retain a structural niche. This moat source appears stable over a multi-year horizon.
- **Reputation**: **Strengthening short-term, cyclically volatile**. Q1 2026’s record results and league table ascent reinforce brand equity. However, advisory reputation is episodic; a failed marquee transaction or a prolonged market downturn could temporarily impair perception. Over a full cycle, the moat is stable, but it is not immune to single-deal tail risk.
- **Human Capital**: **Stable, but requiring continuous reinvestment**. Evercore promotes from within and locks in senior talent with equity, but the entire industry faces key-person risk. The Robey Warshaw acquisition was structured with 50% performance-contingent consideration and multi-year earn-outs explicitly to retain founders [Source: LinkedIn / Title: "Evercore and Robey Warshaw: The Price of Influence in UK Advisory" / Date: 2025-08-18] *. Talent poaching by rival boutiques and bulge brackets remains an active threat. The moat is durable only if compensation pools remain competitive; this is a high-maintenance advantage.
- **Scale Within Boutiques**: **Strengthening**. The Robey Warshaw deal and organic SMD growth suggest increasing breadth. That said, Evercore’s scale is still an order of magnitude smaller than Goldman Sachs or JPMorgan; this moat is relative to other independents, not absolute.

## 3. Competitive Threats

- **Bulge-Bracket Bundling and Balance-Sheet Competition**. Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, and Morgan Stanley retain structural advantages in cross-selling financing, equity underwriting, and lending alongside advisory. In sponsor-backed buyouts requiring committed financing, bulge brackets can bundle debt and M&A advice in ways Evercore cannot. This is an **active threat** for leveraged transactions; it is less severe for pure strategic advisory or special committee roles where conflicts matter most.
- **Lazard’s Diversification Push**. Lazard has agreed to acquire Campbell Lutyens for **$575 million** to create a new private capital advisory division, expected to close in H2 2026 [Source: Private Banker International / Title: "Lazard profit soars 67% in Q1 2026" / Date: 2026-05-04] **. Lazard also benefits from a large asset management business ($259 billion in AUM) that provides revenue ballast Evercore lacks. Over the medium term, Lazard’s push into private capital secondaries could compete for sponsor-related advisory revenue.
- **Rival Boutique Talent Poaching**. Centerview Partners and PJT Partners remain aggressive in recruiting senior bankers with guaranteed compensation packages. If Evercore’s share price or bonus pools underperform, rainmaker defections could rapidly erode client relationships. This is a **latent threat** that activates during compensation downturns.
- **Key-Person Risk in Acquired Platforms**. The Robey Warshaw integration concentrates risk in its founders, Simon Robey (65) and Simon Warshaw (59). If they depart after earn-out periods, UK blue-chip relationships (e.g., BP, Vodafone, HSBC) may weaken [Source: Reuters / Title: "Evercore’s Robey Warshaw deal sums up key-man risk" / Date: 2025-08-01] **. This is a **medium-term threat** (2–5 years) contingent on succession planning.

## 4. Industry Growth & Demand Outlook (1–2 Year Horizon)

### Industry Growth Trajectory
Global M&A advisory fees generally fluctuate between **$30 billion and $40 billion** annually depending on macro conditions, implying a long-term CAGR of roughly 4–6% [Source: KoalaGains / Title: Evercore Stock Analysis / Date: 2026-04-14] *. More authoritative demand forecasts suggest US total deal volume (corporate + PE) will rise **3% in 2026** in a base-case scenario, with upside to **7%** if trade tensions ease and downside to **-3%** under a stagflationary shock [Source: EY / Title: "M&A outlook: stronger US deal market in 2026 despite mixed..." / Date: 2025-10-28] **. Global buyout value surged **44% to $904 billion** in 2025, and sponsor activity is expected to remain elevated [Source: Bain & Company / Title: "Private Equity Outlook 2026: Gaining Traction" / Date: 2026-02-22] **.

### Demand Drivers & Headwinds
**Drivers**: (1) **Private equity exit backlog**: Global PE dry powder stood at approximately **$3.7 trillion** as of early 2026, including **$1.1 trillion** in buyout-specific capital requiring deployment [Source: Pipelineroad / Title: "Private Equity Dry Powder: What $3.7 Trillion Means" referencing Preqin and Bain / Date: 2026-02-10] **; (2) **Private credit capacity**: Direct lenders have demonstrated ability to underwrite multibillion-dollar unitranche facilities, greasing the wheels for sponsor buyouts that syndicated markets might not support [Source: Octus / Title: "Americas Private Credit 2026 Outlook" / Date: 2026-02-02] **; (3) **More permissive US antitrust environment**: Deal blocks and abandonments fell by more than half in 2025, with agencies showing greater willingness to accept remedies [Source: A&O Shearman / Title: "Lighter-touch and fewer M&A blocks for merger control" / Date: 2026-02-25] **.

**Headwinds**: (1) **Sticky inflation and rate stasis**: Core PCE inflation was **3.2%** in March 2026, and the Federal Reserve has held the fed funds rate at **3.50%–3.75%** with markets not pricing cuts until late 2027 [Source: Forbes / Title: "Federal Funds Rate History" / Date: 2026-04-29] **; (2) **Geopolitical volatility**: Oil prices have exceeded **$100 per barrel** due to Middle East conflict, adding uncertainty that can freeze boardroom decision-making [Source: Forbes / Date: 2026-04-29] **; (3) **Increased HSR administrative burden**: A new Hart-Scott-Rodino filing form adopted in February 2025 requires far more extensive disclosure, adding up to a month of preparation time and creating procedural friction [Source: A&O Shearman / Date: 2026-02-25] **.

### Industry Life Cycle & Structural Trends
M&A advisory is a **mature, cyclical industry** currently experiencing a **structural share shift from integrated banks to independent boutiques**. No material non-obvious life cycle commentary is required beyond noting that the boutique segment has gained sustainable share over the past decade as board-level concerns about conflicts have intensified.

## 5. Customer & Supplier Concentration

### Customer Concentration
Evercore completed **806 advisory and underwriting client transactions** in 2025, of which **529 generated fees of at least $1 million** [Source: Company 10-K / Title: 2025 Annual Report / Date: 2026-03-30] ***. No individual client concentration (e.g., top customer exceeding 10% of revenue) is disclosed, suggesting a diversified revenue base across large corporates, financial sponsors, and boards. That said, the top 50 relationships almost certainly drive a disproportionate share of fees, as is typical in relationship-driven advisory. Geographic concentration is more pronounced: the **Americas generated 81.2%** of 2025 net revenues ($3.07 billion), **EMEA 16.6%** ($626 million), and **Asia-Pacific 2.2%** ($83 million) [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Evercore 2025 10-K Filing / Date: 2026-03-30] ***.

### Supplier Dependencies
**Not material for this business model**. Evercore is a human-capital-intensive, knowledge-based advisory firm. Its primary inputs are talent and information services (market data, analytics software), which are available from multiple competing vendors. There is no meaningful supply chain concentration or single-vendor risk.

## 6. Macroeconomic Sensitivities

### Standard Macro Variables
- **Interest Rates**: **Highly relevant**. The fed funds rate at 3.50%–3.75% (as of April 2026) affects sponsor returns and strategic buyer financing costs. Markets expect rate stasis through year-end 2026, with no cuts until late 2027 [Source: Forbes / Title: "Federal Funds Rate History" / Date: 2026-04-29] **. This creates a ceiling on leveraged buyout multiples but also reduces refinancing uncertainty. Evercore’s revenue is positively correlated with M&A velocity, which is inversely correlated with rate volatility.
- **FX**: **Modestly relevant**. With EMEA at ~17% of revenue and Asia-Pacific at ~2%, USD/EUR and USD/GBP fluctuations create translation risk, but the Americas dominance (81%) limits material FX sensitivity [Source: SEC.gov / Title: Evercore 2025 10-K Filing / Date: 2026-03-30] ***.
- **Consumer Spending**: **Immaterial**. Evercore has no consumer-facing revenue.
- **Inflation**: **Indirectly relevant**. Sticky core inflation contributes to the Fed’s hold stance and may pressure compensation costs (banker salaries and bonuses). Energy-driven headline inflation above 4% adds macro uncertainty that can delay board decisions [Source: Forbes / Date: 2026-04-29] **.
- **Energy/Commodity Prices**: **Indirectly relevant only**. Oil >$100/bbl feeds into inflation expectations and geopolitical risk premia, but Evercore has no direct commodity exposure.
- **Labor Markets**: **Highly relevant, inverted**. The relevant labor market is not the general unemployment rate (projected to drift toward 4.8%) but the perennially tight market for senior investment bankers. Compensation is Evercore’s largest expense, totaling **$904 million** in Q1 2026, or **65% of net revenues** [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-04-29] ***.

### Company-Specific Macro Variables
- **M&A Cycle / Deal Velocity**: The single most important macro variable. Evercore’s Q1 2026 advisory fees surged 123% year-over-year, demonstrating extreme operating leverage to deal flow.
- **Private Equity Dry Powder**: The $1.1 trillion in buyout-specific dry powder [Source: Pipelineroad / Date: 2026-02-10] ** is a company-specific demand driver because Evercore derives a significant portion of revenue from financial sponsors.
- **Equity Market Windows**: Evercore’s smaller equities business (commissions, underwriting, and related revenue of ~$118 million in Q1 2026) depends on equity volatility and IPO windows. Geopolitical uncertainty is currently a headwind.
- **Antitrust Regime**: The shift to lighter-touch US merger control reduces deal mortality risk, a direct tailwind for advisory completion rates and fee realization [Source: A&O Shearman / Date: 2026-02-25] **.

## 7. Regulatory Environment

Evercore operates within a multi-jurisdictional regulatory framework that is largely **background compliance** rather than active structural pressure, with a few near-term friction points.

- **US Broker-Dealer and Banking Oversight**: Evercore Group L.L.C. is a US-registered broker-dealer subject to SEC Rule 15c3-1 net capital requirements. As of September 30, 2025, its regulatory net capital was **$346.8 million**, far exceeding minimum requirements [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: Evercore 10-Q Filing / Date: 2025-11-05] ***. Evercore Trust Company, N.A. is regulated by the OCC and subject to Tier 1 capital and liquidity covenants, with which the firm was in compliance as of the same date [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: Evercore 10-Q Filing / Date: 2025-11-05] ***.
- **FINRA Priorities**: FINRA’s 2026 oversight report emphasizes Regulation Best Interest compliance, AI and cybersecurity supervision, and influencer marketing recordkeeping [Source: FINRA / Title: 2026 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report / Date: 2025-12-26] ***. For an institutional advisory firm like Evercore, these are standard compliance matters, though the AI supervision requirements may affect how the firm deploys generative tools.
- **UK and EU Regulation**: Evercore UK and Evercore ISI UK are regulated by the UK Financial Conduct Authority. Aggregate regulatory net capital was **$570.4 million** as of September 30, 2025, exceeding requirements [Source: SEC EDGAR / Title: Evercore 10-Q Filing / Date: 2025-11-05] ***. MiFID II conduct rules and UK SMCR continue to govern remuneration and accountability, but no major 2026 rule changes have been flagged.
- **Antitrust and HSR Friction**: While US merger enforcement has become more business-friendly (no deals were blocked in 2025), the new Hart-Scott-Rodino filing form adopted in February 2025 has significantly increased disclosure burdens. A Texas federal court ruled against the FTC on the form’s cost-benefit analysis, with the agency appealing; clarity is expected in mid-2026 [Source: A&O Shearman / Title: "Lighter-touch and fewer M&A blocks for merger control" / Date: 2026-02-25] **. For Evercore, this is a procedural headwind that lengthens deal timelines but does not threaten advisory demand.

## 8. Technology & Structural Disruption Risks

### Identified Disruption Threats
1. **Generative AI in Front-Office Banking**. AI tools are automating document review, financial data extraction, first-draft pitch book creation, and basic valuation modeling. Industry estimates project front-office productivity improvements of **27–35% by 2026** for firms that adopt these tools aggressively [Source: Boston Institute of Analytics / Title: "The Rise Of AI In Investment Banking" / Date: 2026-04-24] *. If realized, this could reduce analyst headcount needs and compress the traditional investment banking pyramid.
2. **AI-Enabled Deal Sourcing and Target Screening**. Natural language processing and machine learning platforms can now screen thousands of potential acquisition targets, reducing the traditional origination role of junior bankers [Source: The USA Journals / Title: "Redefining Entry-Level Analyst Roles in M&A" / Date: 2025-07-23] * (note: lower-credibility academic-style source). The threat is latent; while AI aids origination, board-level mandates remain governed by senior relationships and trust.
3. **Private Credit and Direct Lending Expansion**. While not a disruption to the advisory model itself, the rise of private credit—now capable of underwriting **$10 billion** unitranche transactions—reduces reliance on syndicated leveraged finance and bulge-bracket lending desks [Source: Octus / Title: "Americas Private Credit 2026 Outlook" / Date: 2026-02-02] **. This indirectly supports the independent advisory model by enabling sponsor deals without bundled bank financing.

### Timeline & Probability Assessment
- **Generative AI productivity impact**: **Near-term (0–2 years), High probability**. The tools are already deployed across major banks. Probability is high that analyst headcount-per-deal declines and margin structures shift. For Evercore, this is likely a margin tailwind rather than a revenue threat in the near term.
- **AI-enabled disintermediation of senior advisory**: **Long-term (5+ years), Low probability**. Would require corporate boards to trust algorithmic judgment over human relationships for fiduciary-grade M&A decisions. Given the reputational and legal stakes, this remains unlikely.
- **Private credit structural shift**: **Medium-term (2–5 years), High probability**. Private credit is already facilitating large sponsor buyouts. This supports advisory volume by expanding the universe of financeable transactions.

### Company Response & Adaptation
Evercore has not publicly detailed a firm-wide AI strategy in its earnings disclosures. However, technology and information services spending rose to **$40.4 million** in Q1 2026 (up from $33.4 million in Q1 2025) [Source: Company 8-K / Title: Q1 2026 Earnings Release / Date: 2026-04-29] ***, suggesting incremental investment in digital infrastructure. The firm continues to prioritize senior banker hiring (182 IB SMDs) over headcount reduction, implying that AI is currently viewed as augmenting, not replacing, its talent-centric model.

## 9. Bottom-Line Competitive & Macro Assessment

### Competitive Position Characterization
Evercore is the **dominant independent M&A advisory firm globally**, currently operating at a cyclical peak with Q1 2026 results that rival bulge-bracket investment banking revenue. Its conflict-free model, reputation for mega-cap execution, and expanded EMEA footprint via Robey Warshaw create a defensible niche at the top of the advisory value chain. The firm is not invulnerable: it remains a transaction-revenue business with no recurring income buffer, and its human-capital moat requires continuous, expensive maintenance.

### Key Competitive & Macro Drivers (confidence weighted)
1. **Global M&A deal cycle and private equity exit activity** — **High confidence**. Evercore’s Q1 2026 advisory fee surge (123% YoY) demonstrates acute cyclical leverage. PE dry powder ($1.1 trillion buyout-specific) and a more permissive US antitrust environment support continued activity into late 2026.
2. **Independence premium versus bulge-bracket conflicts** — **High confidence**. The secular share shift toward independent advisors is structural. Evercore’s five-year track record as the leading independent by announced volume validates this positioning.
3. **Talent retention and compensation dynamics** — **High confidence**. Compensation consumes ~65% of net revenue. Poaching risks and key-man dependency (especially in the UK) are permanent features of the business model.
4. **Private credit as deal enabler** — **Medium confidence**. Direct lending capacity supports large sponsor transactions, indirectly driving advisory demand, but credit spreads remain vulnerable to macro shocks.

### Key Competitive & Macro Risks (confidence weighted)
1. **Macro/geopolitical shock freezing boardroom activity** — **High confidence**. Elevated oil prices (>$100/bbl) and Middle East conflict could trigger a sudden M&A freeze. Evercore has no recurring revenue to cushion a collapse in transaction volume.
2. **Bulge-bracket bundling recapturing share** — **Medium confidence**. In sponsor-backed buyouts requiring committed financing, bulge brackets can leverage balance-sheet lending to win back lead advisory roles. This risk is most acute in leveraged transactions and less severe in pure strategic advisory.
3. **Failed integration or key-person departure post-acquisition** — **Medium confidence**. The Robey Warshaw deal added elite UK relationships but concentrated risk in aging founders. Succession failures could impair EMEA growth.
4. **AI-driven commoditization of junior banking work** — **Low confidence (near-term), Medium confidence (long-term)**. Near term, AI is a margin tool. Long term, if generative AI replaces analyst-level diligence and modeling at scale, the traditional pyramid model of investment banks may need restructuring, potentially affecting talent pipelines.

### Catalysts to Watch
- **Late July 2026**: Evercore Q2 2026 earnings release. Will confirm whether Q1’s $1.25 billion advisory revenue represented a sustainable run-rate or a pull-forward of mega-deals.
- **August 2026**: Expected clarity on the FTC appeal regarding the new HSR filing form. A rollback would reduce deal friction; an FTC victory would prolong administrative delays [Source: A&O Shearman / Date: 2026-02-25] **.
- **September 17–18, 2026**: Federal Reserve September FOMC meeting. If the Fed signals cuts despite sticky inflation, M&A financing conditions could ease. If rates remain on hold, the "rate stasis" headwind persists.
- **H2 2026**: Expected completion of Lazard’s Campbell Lutyens acquisition, creating a new private capital advisory competitor [Source: Private Banker International / Date: 2026-05-04] **.
- **Ongoing**: Any departure of Simon Robey or Simon Warshaw from Evercore’s UK platform post-earnout.

## 10. Data Gaps & Limitations

1. **Primary League Table Data**: Q1 2026 global and Americas M&A league table rankings were sourced from LinkedIn and Wall Street Oasis posts referencing Dealogic, not directly from Dealogic, Bloomberg, Refinitiv, or official industry associations. **Search scope**: Dealogic public reports, Bloomberg Professional Services press releases, Refinitiv league table announcements. **Analytical impact**: Market share quantification is tentative and should be verified with a primary data provider.
2. **FX Exposure and Hedging Strategy**: No specific quantitative disclosure on foreign exchange translation impact, currency hedging, or sensitivity analysis was located beyond geographic revenue breakdown. **Search scope**: Evercore 10-K risk factors, earnings call transcripts, investor presentations. **Analytical impact**: FX sensitivity assessment remains qualitative.
3. **Customer Concentration**: Evercore does not disclose customer concentration metrics (e.g., top 10 clients as percentage of revenue) in public filings, only transaction counts. **Search scope**: 2025 10-K, 10-Qs, supplemental earnings disclosures. **Analytical impact**: Revenue concentration risk cannot be precisely quantified.
4. **AI Strategy Disclosure**: No direct disclosure from Evercore on specific AI vendors, headcount impact targets, or automation initiatives was found. **Search scope**: Evercore investor relations, SEC filings, Q1 2026 earnings call transcript. **Analytical impact**: AI disruption assessment relies on industry-wide projections rather than firm-specific evidence.
5. **Stale Industry Forecasts**: Some consensus deal-volume forecasts (e.g., EY M&A outlook) are dated October 2025 and may not fully reflect the Q1 2026 deal surge or the April 2026 escalation in Middle East conflict and oil prices. **Search scope**: EY, Deloitte, McKinsey updated Q2 2026 outlooks. **Analytical impact**: Base-case growth assumptions carry higher uncertainty.
